sections.paragraph:
ἀϰούω [etym. complex]

—1. primary sense hear Mt 13:9, 13; with focus on willingness to listen to or heed the substance of what is said 17:5; Ac 28:28.

—2. ‘hear with comprehension’, understand 1 Cor 14:2; Gal 4:21.

—3. ‘receive information aurally’, hear, hear about Mt 14:13; Ro 10:18; pass. be said/rumored 1 Cor 5:1; with focus on receipt of specific instruction learn 1 J 1:5 al.

—4. Legal term: hear a case, grant a hearing J 7:51; Ac 25:22.
